commit.message:
fix: correct iyzico V2 authorization signature calculation

The hash payload was incorrectly using apiKey+randomKey+secretKey+body
instead of randomKey+uri+body, and HMAC result was not being used in
the final authorization header.

- Add founder/author Person schema to all service and location +Head.tsx files
- Add lastmod dates to all sitemap entries and include missing pages (about, privacy-policy, terms-of-business)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: load Eunomia vocabulary tables from GiBleed zip (don't skip)

Previously VOCAB_TABLES were skipped under the assumption that concept,
concept_ancestor, etc. already exist in an 'omop' schema. This assumption
only held on the Acumenus dev environment. On a fresh Docker install there
is no omop schema so concept tables were empty, breaking vocab search and
Abby AI concept lookup.

The GiBleed zip includes all vocabulary CSVs — load them into the eunomia
schema alongside the clinical tables.

commit.message:
Fix Google Calendar not detected after OAuth redirect

The SWR cache for /api/google-calendar/status was stale after the
OAuth callback redirected to /dashboard?gcal=connected. Now detect
the query param on mount, re-fetch status, and trigger sync for
the current week when isConnected flips to true.

commit.message:
Delay post-save follow-up nudge, cancel if curator starts typing

Show "✓ Saved." immediately, then wait 3 seconds before adding a
follow-up nudge. If the curator starts typing during that window,
cancel the nudge. Nudge messages vary by rec count.

commit.message:
fix: use non-blocking executor shutdown for agent timeout

The previous ThreadPoolExecutor context manager waited for hung threads.
Use shutdown(wait=False) and recreate the executor after a timeout so the
orchestrator can continue to the next agent and run fusion.

commit.message:
feat: serial benchmark infrastructure for Jetson Orin

Jetson's 7.4 GB unified memory is shared between CPU and GPU —
running multiple servers simultaneously causes memory contention
and invalidates benchmark results.

New per-runtime forjar configs stop ALL other servers before starting:
- forjar-jetson-realizr.yaml
- forjar-jetson-ollama.yaml
- forjar-jetson-llamacpp.yaml

New Makefile targets:
- make bench-jetson-serial    (all 3 runtimes, c=1 + c=4, isolated)
- make bench-jetson-realizr   (realizr only)
- make bench-jetson-ollama    (ollama only)
- make bench-jetson-llamacpp  (llama.cpp only)

Refs PMAT-020

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update footer color and right-align mobile hamburger

- Footer bg: #101828 → #202124

commit.message:
test: increase unit test coverage from ~55% to ~60% (#146)

Add 166 new test cases across 6 new test files targeting previously
uncovered modules. All new tests pass and use only mocks/stubs with
no live browser, network, or LLM calls.

New test files:
- tests/test_exceptions.py: full hierarchy coverage for all 7 exception
  classes in src/exceptions.py (was 0%, now 100%)
- tests/api/test_routes_extended.py: GET /api/providers, GET /api/info,
  POST /jobs/{id}/pause, POST /jobs/{id}/resume, POST /jobs/resume-from-state,
  GET /jobs/{id}/events 404 branch, GET /api/models?provider=... parameter
  (routes.py coverage: 59% -> 82%)
- tests/llm/test_client_extended.py: get_available_models() caching,
  _get_openrouter_models() success/error paths, _generate_openrouter() and
  _generate_opencode() with/without API keys, generate() routing,
  legacy wrapper functions (client.py coverage: 45% -> 94%)
- tests/llm/test_cleanup_extended.py: _code_density(), _has_latex() edge
  cases, classify_chunk() all branches, _cleanup_options() large content,
  _estimate_tokens() all three ratio paths (cleanup.py: 96% -> 98%)
- tests/scraper/test_markdown_extended.py: _pre_clean_markdown() JS/CSS
  block masking, _chunk_by_size() overlap and boundary logic, chunk_markdown()
  edge cases, _chunk_by_headings() tiny section exclusion (markdown.py: 85% -> 98%)
- tests/scraper/test_structured_extended.py: table extraction, <pre> without
  <code>, container tag recursion, content area selection, fallback paragraph
  threshold, image edge cases, save_structured() edge cases
  (structured.py: 84% -> 97%)

Coverage delta: 55.14% -> ~60% total

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive error handling for OpenAI API calls in workout plan generation

- Wrap OpenAI API call in try-catch with specific error handling
- Return user-friendly error messages for common scenarios:
  - Rate limiting (503): "AI service experiencing high demand"
  - Authentication failures (500): "AI service configuration error"
  - Timeout errors (504): "AI service took too long to respond"
  - Model unavailable (503): "AI model currently unavailable"
  - Token limit exceeded (400): "Request too large"
- Log detailed error context for debugging (status, type, code, message)
- Replace generic "Failed to generate workout plan" with actionable guidance
- Handle empty OpenAI responses with proper error message

Fixes production 500 errors by providing specific error handling instead of
letting OpenAI errors bubble up to generic catch block.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Wave 96: health history tracking, SSE reconnect backoff, health persistence

- New health_history table in MasterDB (repo_id, date, score, grade) with daily UPSERT
- save_health_scores() persists to DB on every /api/health/detailed call
- get_health_history(days) returns historical health data
- GET /api/health/history?days=30 endpoint for health trend data

- sseRetries ref tracks reconnection attempts, resets on success

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SIGABRT crash from ggml_abort in llama_context::decode

Increase n_ctx from 2048 → 4096 in ask_saul(). The previous limit caused
llama.cpp to call ggml_abort() (SIGABRT, uncatchable) whenever legal
document context pushed the prompt past 2048 tokens.

Two-layer protection added:
1. query(): pre-truncate document context strings to ≤13 000 chars before
   building the prompt, keeping the system instructions and user question intact
2. ask_saul(): post-tokenization token count guard — if tokens still exceed
   n_ctx - 512, drain from the prompt start (preserves user question at end)

Also: max_new_tokens 1024 → 2048 for more complete legal answers.

use Rosetta 2.

Phase 3 P2-P5 — UX planning, statistiques, pauses vendeurs, PWA

- P2 : Vue semaine, duplication journées, présence en masse, badges statut
- P3 : Archivage auto à la clôture, dashboard stats semaine/mois, classement vendeurs, export CSV
- P4 : Pause/reprise vendeurs, exclusion du calcul de priorité, durée de pause loggée
- P5 : PWA installable (manifest, service worker vanilla, meta iOS, icônes relais, optimisation tactile)

Post-refactor continuity audit: fix all 14 issues from #20

Errors:
- Ch. 24: slew date day 347 → approximately day 345
- Ch. 23/24: near-Earth swarm comparison "entire" → "significant fraction"
- Ch. 7: staleness factor 600× → 8× (600h/72h)
- World bible: "volumiMira" typo → "voluminous"
- World bible: complete Mira→Iris rename (~30 occurrences)

Moderate:
- Prologue: light delay "fourteen" → "thirteen" (4 hops × 3.2 min)
- Ch. 19: §5.2 → §5.2.2 (IMR + dispatch)
- Chs. 16/17: severance duration 26 → 31-32 days
- Ch. 25: gimbal exceedance 14 → 10 hours

Minor:
- Ch. 22: "40 days" → 41 days (338-297=41)
- World bible: CVDF-1 15-20% → 15-19%
- Vote circuit: 36m20s → 30m58s (matches 3.27 min/hop physics)
- IMR headers: standardize ENTRY format, add calendar lines, replace
  raw timestamps with placeholders (5 new timestamp IDs)
- File naming: resolve 03_ prefix collision by renumbering ch03+ by +1

Add auto-work feature: auto-trigger work stage after plan success

Adds a global setting and per-task override to automatically trigger
the work stage when a plan completes successfully. Task-level flag
(true/false/null) takes precedence over the global setting.

Backend: new auto_work field on Task model, auto-work logic in runner
after plan success with max_active_agents enforcement, updated API.
Frontend: auto_work toggle on Settings page, per-task dropdown on
TaskDetail (Default/On/Off), new useAutoWork hook.
Tests: 4 backend tests covering all precedence cases, 2 frontend tests.

commit.message:
Fix mobile TouchGui destroyed by React reconciler

React's createRoot(playerGui) was taking ownership of PlayerGui and
destroying all children it didn't create (TouchGui, Freecam, dialog)
on first render. Fixed by mounting React into a Folder and using
createPortal to render ScreenGuis into PlayerGui without claiming
ownership. Also fixed TouchControlsEnabled effect to skip initial
mount to avoid racing PlayerModule initialization.

Includes: DialogService integration, NPC dialog triggers, overlay
system updates, multiplayer room fixes, and latest Studio sync.
